item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could we maybe not do this in the comments section of a skip I wrote years ago? :p there's enough discussion of this going around in others places, we don't need to re-hash it all here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the idea of linking to translations in the sidebar is cool

That's something that was done site-wide awhile ago. It's automatically generated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All right, I need to address this: I hate the nu-SCP theme. I can appreciate wanting to make the site more accessible, but this theme is aesthetically and functionally flawed, for a few reasons:

  • The color that links change to when they're clicked on is already hard for me to see on my devices, and this somehow makes it worse.
  • It isn't immediately evident that the sidebar is meant to scroll on desktops and laptops; I legitimately thought the red bar that acts as the scroll indicator (or whatever the proper name is) was just to highlight the top few things. It's worse on smaller screens, like laptop monitors.
  • The fact that the dropdown lists at the top are translucent is really not a good thing, especially the canon and GOI tabs. Like, what's the point of making them see through? It just serves to make them harder to read.
  • It's overall too brutalist. I realize that the Foundation doesn't have the most cuddly aesthetic, but making everything hard corners is just off-putting. And the colors suck; I get it, simple color scheme is easier for colorblind people to read, points for that, but to someone who has good color vision, even adjusting the angle of a screen slightly can make the entire thing look like a massive grey mess. Plus, the deep reds clash with pretty much any other color you could put on the page, including colored photographs.

There are some things I like about it, but even those have problems; the idea of linking to translations in the sidebar is cool, but are we expected to put those in ourselves? If so, how? And the more compact tabs at the top are an amazing idea, but again, as-is, they're poorly implemented.

As it stands, this theme needs a lot of work, and I can't really support it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm using the article CSS "Nu-SCP", a new layout being made by Kaktus, Woedenaz, Rounderhouse and Croquemboche. Several people are adopting it as a cleaner alternative to the rather drab and cluttered current theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the layout of the site different on this article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the authorship is no longer clear from this: this skip was written by me, Tufto, on my first account prior to my deleting it. You can find more of my stuff here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent skip, while Isfahan was an excellent city, too.

I have translate it into Chinese.

Yet, I'm curious about the meaning of Jews and Rostam, what do "Unknown" mean?

 
 

作者也太精神萨法维了吧！

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is indeed, at least as far as the Foundation knows. My headcanon is that something weird happens on every significant anniversary of the Fall of Isfahan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite my total lack of any cultural standpoint to interpret them by, these Iranian culture skips have been marvelous. I take it that final event was just something completely anomalous, even within the rules of the anomaly itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really great. Besides the very powerful core image, which compelled me to upvote by the end of the description, I also thought that the decision to mostly stick to clinical supplemental documents (except for the letter, which is short and missing information) highlighted the article's strengths. All of the images in the incident log are vivid and well thought-out; describing them in sparse clinical language lets the strength of those images speak for themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember the first time that I read this, I was surprised that the ORIA allowed this to happen. Then, I read it a bit more closely and realized why. I feel as though there is a lot of potential in the "discovery of modern mnestics" by the Foundation.

I also appreciate the changes you made, and am very glad that this exists. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its perfection goes beyond the mere requirements of the city, but is instead designed to simulate a particular alchemical representation of the human mind.

Alllllchemy eh? :D

+1, enjoyed this.

item.title: Great job Tufto!
item.content:encoded: I liked this when it was a draft and I like it even more now as a fully developed article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed a few errors I spotted, hope that's ok!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that is certainly something new to me to take note of in my future writing. Thanks. You got 1+ from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, it's meant to be mnestics, the memory-restoring drug :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dose of mnestics

Might have a typo there at the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Half the World" is a common nickname for Isfahan, hence the title.

With thanks to the following for reading it over:

SpookyPizza
Modern_Erasmus
Sterbai
Ellied
Eirwen
Anaxagoras
SunnyClockwork
MrWrong
Gaffney
Weryllium

The picture is from here, and is in the public domain.
